core_concept: Climate Zones: Hot, Cold, Wet, and Dry Regions of Earth
facts: Places near the equator are hot all year because the sun shines directly on them, while places near the poles are cold all year because the sun's rays hit them at a slant.
A desert is a dry region that gets very little rain, so it has few plants and animals that can survive without much water.
A tropical rainforest is a hot and humid zone that gets rain almost every day, which helps many different kinds of plants and animals live there.
misconceptions: Students may believe that deserts have no plants or animals at all, because they only see pictures of empty sand dunes.
